Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, low urine specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, suggesting possible renal impairment. There is also bilirubinuria and mild ketonuria. Blood glucose is normal, but HbA1c is unexpectedly low. Lipid profile reveals elevated total and LDL cholesterol. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for underlying causes of renal dysfunction and dyslipidemia.
